title: Service Delivery and Contractor Management
shortSummary: Run contractors well: scopes, expectations, supervision, documentation, and performance conversations.
whyItMatters: Most property outcomes are delivered through third parties. If the contractor relationship is unclear, inconsistent, or weakly governed, the asset absorbs the consequences: missed SLAs, tenant frustration, safety exposure, and budget leakage. Strong contractor management turns outsourced work into reliable building performance.
coreConcepts: How to set and communicate operating standards
The difference between instructing, supervising, and performance-managing vendors
The documentation trail you need when service slips
